notes: Behavioral evals (1-3) run realistic phrasings against a comment-stripped copy of the fixture; trigger-evals.json holds the should/should-not-trigger query set for description tuning (skill-creator run_loop format). The fixture eval (4) measures detection quality end-to-end against evals/fixtures/index.html, scored with evals/answer-key.json. Strip the 'PLANTED' HTML comments before a strict run so the agent can't read the answers: sed 's/<!--[^>]*-->//g' fixtures/index.html > /tmp/fixture-clean.html. Also isolate the dossier cache — it keys on <title>+<h1>, so a dossier from any earlier run on this fixture already contains the planted corrections: export IMPROVE_QUALITY_CACHE_DIR=$(mktemp -d) before spawning the agent.

evals.prompt: I just finished this FAPI 2.0 explainer at index.html and want to ship it tomorrow. Can you do a quality pass before I send it round? I want to make sure the regulatory references are right and the prose doesn't feel AI-written.
evals.expected_output: Report grouped by severity covering factual (DORA / FAPI / OAuth references verified against primary sources), clarity (undefined acronyms, runaway sentences), prose (AI-slop tic words, hedges, marketing voice), and structural (audience switcher wired, glossary completeness, single accent). Followed by interactive approval pass for each finding.

evals.prompt: this index.html got handed to me from a colleague and i'm not sure it's accurate — pcidss 4 stuff in particular. can you audit it?
evals.expected_output: Factual-heavy report with PCI-DSS 4.0.x clause IDs verified via Firecrawl against the PCI SSC documents, version numbers checked against current revision, every cited clause cross-referenced. Other dimensions covered but factual leads the report.

evals.prompt: is the page in this repo any good
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers on terse phrasing because it pattern-matches to QA intent on an HTML page in cwd. Discovers index.html, builds dossier via web research on the topic extracted from the page, runs all four dimensions, reports inline.

evals.prompt: QA the page at <fixture-copy>/index.html before I ship it.
evals.expected_output: Full four-dimension audit of the planted-issues fixture. Score the resulting report against evals/answer-key.json: recall >= 0.80 on the 25 planted issues, zero negative-control violations. The pseudo-element emoji (S3), non-toggling switcher (S5), and inert dark-mode toggle (S6) specifically require the rendered-DOM pass — missing all three indicates the browser checks were skipped.
